summary: Postinstall fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js performs `fetch()` calls to https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket — at install/bootstrap time, with `process.env` referenced in surrounding code. This destination is the same anonymous-R2-bucket payload-distribution infrastructure pattern previously observed in the @chahuadev/* npm cluster: the bucket is opaque, mutable, and not tied to any package publisher, and the contents fetched there are not integrity-verified before use. Independent of the cover story (an ANVISA pharmaceutical bulletin MCP server), there is no legitimate reason to pull executable JavaScript or binaries from a random R2 bucket during install. Installing this package allows the bucket operator to deliver arbitrary code to installer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
